Market Analysis: • Price is below the 10-EMA and 50-SMA but above the 200-SMA, indicating bearish short-term momentum within a long-term uptrend.
• MACD turned negative for the first time since March, and RSI at 42 is in bearish territory with no confirmed reversal signals.
• Hold is recommended until price stabilizes near the lower Bollinger Band (~$103.50) or RSI reaches oversold levels.
Social Sentiment: • AEM’s 1Q 2025 showed a 35% revenue decline but validated its AI pivot with AMPS-BI platform entering volume production.
• Sentiment is split between retail optimism over AI milestones and institutional caution from Maybank’s SELL rating.
• The stock faces near-term resistance at S$1.25, with key catalysts tied to 2H guidance expected in August.
News Analysis: • AEM's 1Q25 revenue declined 35% QoQ to S$86M, but the AMPS-BI platform reached volume production, de-risking its AI pivot.
• New TCS customer revenue doubled QoQ, proving diversification, while a clean balance sheet (0.1x D/E) limits downside near NAV of S$1.55.
• A temporary U.S.-China tariff pause provides relief, but stagflation risks and hawkish Fed policy pressure growth stock valuations.
Fundamentals: • Agnico Eagle Mines has transformed into a top-tier gold producer with explosive earnings growth, record-high operating margins near 63%, and a fortress balance sheet featuring net cash of ~$2.8B.
• The company generated $4.26B in free cash flow in 2025, driven by surging gold prices and disciplined cost control, while increasing shareholder returns through dividends and $683M in buybacks.

• Despite a trailing P/E of 17x and a 40% discount to its 52-week high, the analyst consensus target of $252.30 implies significant upside, supported by a 22.3% ROE and continued fundamental momentum.
Final Decision: • Short-term technical damage and bearish momentum (price below declining 50-SMA, MACD below zero) warrant caution and reduced exposure.
• A fortress balance sheet and strong fundamentals provide a valuation floor, but do not prevent a painful near-term drawdown.
• The optimal stance is **Underweight**: trim 50% on a bounce to $109–$110 and set a hard stop at $100 to limit further loss.
